title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

title: Honda and QuantumScape (QS) team up to make solid-state batteries
description: Honda and QuantumScape (QS) announced a new partnership to commercialize solid-state batteries for EVs and more.

title: Before SpaceX IPO, investors in China secretly acquired stakes

description: One previously unreported SpaceX investor has ties to Chinese military contractors. The information was revealed only after ProPublica went to court to obtain it.

title: Waymo Recalls Robotaxis Over Risk They'll Drive at Speed Into Freeway Construction Zones
description: The company's latest recall of 3,871 vehicles follows incidents of its autonomous cars prioritizing other hazards or failing to recognize closed construction zones altogether.

title: Richtech Robotics launches livestream for ADAM AI-powered humanoid
description:
Online users will be able to engage with service robot ADAM, which uses NVIDIA technology, says Richtech Robotics.
The post Richtech Robotics launches livestream for ADAM AI-powered humanoid appeared first on The Robot Report.

title: Waymo recalls nearly 4,000 robotaxis to stop them driving into highway construction zones
description: The company has identified at least 13 instances where its robotaxis drove into highway sections closed for construction.

title: Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad
description:
Blue Origin has started rebuilding a launch pad severely damaged in a New Glenn explosion less than three weeks ago as it works to resume launches by year’s end
The post Blue Origin begins rebuilding New Glenn pad appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Cargofy lands $6M to scale AI workers for logistics
description: Logisticstechnology company Cargofy has raised $11 million in a Series A funding round,consisting of $6 million in primary capital and $5 million in secondarytransactions. The round was led by u.ventu...

title: ‘This will unveil the ground truth’: Why the Anthropic ban could benefit European AI
The Korean Telecom Giant at the Center of Anthropic’s Mythos Controversy
description: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.

title: Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission
description:
Relativity Space plans to launch a Mars orbiter in 2028 as part of a new initiative to privately develop planetary missions.
The post Relativity Space to privately develop Mars orbiter mission appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Operating a Humanoid With Your Body Is a Hot Job in China’s Hardware Capital
description: In Shenzhen, workers at IO-AI Tech control humanoid robots using a VR rig reminiscent of Ready Player One.

title: DOJ Rushes To Help xAI Pollute The Skies Over Mississippi
description:
xAI is hiding behind the skirts of Uncle Sam and the DOJ to dodge its legal obligations not to poison people with its activities. 
The post DOJ Rushes To Help xAI Pollute The Skies Over Mississippi appeared first on CleanTechnica.

title: Trump’s DOJ intervenes to keep Musk’s xAI gas turbines polluting Memphis
description:
The US Department of Justice has asked a federal court to throw out a Clean Air Act lawsuit against Elon Musk’s xAI, arguing that the company’s unpermitted gas turbines near Memphis are a matter of “national, economic, and energy security.”

title: Autonomique deploys semi-humanoid robots and AI at Canadian Tier 1
description:
Autonomique has moved its AI-powered robots from pilot to production at F&P Mfg., with plans for deployment across F-tech's global network.
The post Autonomique deploys semi-humanoid robots and AI at Canadian Tier 1 appeared first on The Robot Report.

title: The Secret to Marathon-Winning Humanoid Robots
description: On April 19, 2026, the Honor Lightning humanoid robot ran a half-marathon in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, beating the human world record by 7 minutes and the best robot time from 2025 by almost two hours.How did they do it? Is there some magical technology or technique that unlocked this performance?

title: Former Palantir employees raise $60M for AI enterprise startup Conduct
description: A London-based AI startup founded by three former Palantir employees, which is looking to modernise ERP systems, has raised $60m in new funding. The Series A investment in Conduct was co-led by new in...

title: Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million
description:
Dawn Aerospace has raised $25 million to scale up its work in both in-space transportation and suborbital spaceplanes.
The post Dawn Aerospace raises $25 million appeared first on SpaceNews.

title: Mistral CEO pitches open source AI days after Anthropic ban: ‘We exist outside of state control’
Dozens of dust devils hidden in plain sight
description: The European Space Agency’s Mars Express has captured part of Mars’s Mamers Valles: a fascinating valley system speckled with brief, tornado-like whirlwinds known as dust devils.

title: Waymo taps fleet giant Element to scale its robotaxi service
description: Waymo has tapped fleet management company Element to help keep its robotaxis charged, maintained, and on the road as the company expands into more cities.

title: ChatGPT to debut on Pentagon's GenAI.mil in ‘early July’, OpenAI says
description: It will be the latest model available for sensitive but unclassified work on the platform.

title: SpaceX Acquires AI Coding Tool Cursor For $60B In Year’s Largest Startup M&A Deal
description: The acquisition of Cursor gives SpaceX a foothold into the enterprise software development market, where AI-assisted coding has taken off and led large companies to significantly pare back their reliance on human engineers.

title: Industry and academia call on administration to free Anthropic’s AI model
description: 30-plus industry and academic professionals signed a letter citing international competition and the need to patch network vulnerabilities.

title: Built Robotics, Penn xLAB to develop physical AI for construction
description:
xLAB and Built Robotics partner to capture additional data, advancing AI models to improve construction site safety.

title: PSYONIC partners with ABB Robotics to apply human touch to robot dexterity
description:
PSYONIC is combining its Ability Hand with ABB Robotics' GoFa cobot arm to apply data from prosthetics users to robotic grasping.
The post PSYONIC partners with ABB Robotics to apply human touch to robot dexterity appeared first on The Robot Report.

title: Autonomous freight developer Einride goes public via SPAC
description:
Stockholm-based Einride operates a fleet of heavy-duty electric trucks servicing Global Fortune 500 companies across the U.S. and Europe.

title: Anthropic suspends top AI models after US export-control order
description: U.S. officials ordered the company to limit foreign access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.

title: Waymo launches $30/month ‘Premier’ membership with priority pickups and cashback
description:
Waymo is launching its first-ever membership program, “Waymo Premier,” offering its most frequent riders priority pickups, 10% cashback, and early access to new cities for $29.99 per month.

title: Contriving Imaginary Gaps in Nuclear Deterrence
description: Among some nuclear strategists, military officers, and lawmakers, a belief bordering on the canonical has taken root that the United States is on the short end of a “deterrence gap” with Russia and China. title: When Refusal Doesn’t Matter: Operation Epic Fury and the Erosion of Host Nation Consent
description: When the U.S. military launched its war against Iran in Feb. 2026, it did not just dismantle Iranian military capabilities. It shattered the illusion that the United States would consult with its closest allies and that an ally’s refusal to grant base access can stop an American war in motion.

Jazz, AbCellera strike TCE discovery and development deal worth up to $820M per drug
description: Jazz Pharmaceuticals is looking to bolster its oncology research bona fides via a partnership with AbCellera to discover and develop T-cell-engaging (TCE) antibodies that could result in up to $820 million in biobucks for each program that Jazz options.
